query:
INSERT INTO public.questions (
    id, question_text, question_type, options, correct_answer, explanation, hint,
    stimulus_text, stimulus_type, section, topic, difficulty, exam_type,
    is_platform_question, ai_generated, calculator_allowed, subject, subtopic,
    math_domain, pool_key, diagnostic_eligible, tags, external_id, source, source_metadata
  ) VALUES
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author suggests that the red-winged blackbird’s song is',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"a complex and beautiful melody that is meant to be appreciated by humans.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"a simple and repetitive melody that is effective in attracting attention and reassuring listeners.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"a reflection of the bird’s natural environment and its relationship with other birds.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"a complex and unpredictable melody that is difficult to interpret.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The author emphasizes the simple, repetitive nature of the red-winged blackbird’s song and how it serves a specific purpose: to attract attention and reassure listeners.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author describes a particular type of bird, the red-winged blackbird, as having a “peculiarly melodious song” that is “characteristically simple and repetitive.” The author describes the song’s simplicity as “a way of attracting attention” and its repetitiveness as “a way of reassuring the listener.” What is the main idea of this passage?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'random_id_a6',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author uses several examples to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"create a sense of urgency and excitement.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"develop a central theme and emphasize its importance.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"provide a detailed account of the author’s personal experiences.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"suggest that the author has a unique perspective on the world.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The author uses several examples to develop a central theme and emphasize its importance. The examples of resilience are all different, but they all support the author’s message that resilience is a valuable quality.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author uses several examples to illustrate the theme of resilience. The author describes how a young girl overcomes a difficult childhood and achieves success in her career. The author also depicts a community that rebuilds after a natural disaster. Finally, the author tells the story of a man who perseveres through a challenging illness and finds hope. How does the author use these examples?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Craft and Structure']::text[],
    'random_id_b1',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Craft and Structure","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author most likely mentions the changes in transportation and the growth of suburbs in order to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"show the reader the widespread use of cars in the early 1900s.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"explain how the rise of the automobile led to a number of social and cultural changes.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"argue that the rise of the automobile had a negative impact on urban areas.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"compare the benefits of using cars to the benefits of using horse-drawn carriages.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The author is discussing the social and cultural changes that resulted from the rise of the automobile. The changes in transportation and the growth of suburbs are examples of these social and cultural changes.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'In the early 1900s, as cars became more common in urban areas, the number of horse-drawn carriages began to decline.  The rise of the automobile changed the way people traveled, but it also led to a number of social and cultural changes. For example, the widespread use of cars led to the development of new forms of transportation, such as taxi cabs and bus lines.  Cars also contributed to the growth of suburbs, as people could now live farther away from their jobs.  What is the most likely reason why the author mentions the changes in transportation and the growth of suburbs?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'f74512b3',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The phrase "serene and winding ribbon of water" is used to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"create a sense of wonder and awe at the beauty of the natural world.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"highlight the narrator’s sense of loneliness and isolation.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"emphasize the contrast between the natural world and the city.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"suggest that the narrator is feeling anxious and uncertain.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The phrase "serene and winding ribbon of water" creates a sense of wonder and awe at the beauty of the natural world. The word "serene" implies calmness and peace, while the phrase "winding ribbon" suggests a sense of graceful movement and beauty. The image of the river flowing through the landscape evokes a sense of tranquility and harmony, which is in line with the overall tone of the passage.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'While on his journey through the countryside, the narrator is struck by the beauty of his surroundings, particularly the river. He describes the river as a “serene and winding ribbon of water” that flows through the landscape, “a constant companion” to his travels. The narrator is also drawn to the birds that he sees, describing their songs as “sweet melodies that fill the air.” This description of the natural world conveys a sense of peace and tranquility, a feeling of being at one with nature. What is the most likely reason why the author uses the phrase “serene and winding ribbon of water” to describe the river?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'a1d27d47',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice most effectively combines the sentences at the underlined portion to create a grammatically correct and stylistically effective sentence?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"the team had a long losing streak; and the coach was criticized for his leadership, the team’s win was also significant","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"the team had a long losing streak, and the coach was criticized for his leadership; the team’s win was also significant","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"the team had a long losing streak; and the coach was criticized for his leadership, the team’s win was also significant,","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"the team had a long losing streak, and the coach was criticized for his leadership, the team’s win was also significant","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'Choice B is the best answer because it correctly combines the two sentences using a semicolon, which can be used to join two independent clauses that are closely related in meaning. The comma in Choice A creates a comma splice, which is grammatically incorrect. Choices C and D are incorrect because a semicolon should not be followed by a comma.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The team’s victory was unexpected; the team had a long losing streak, and the coach was criticized for his leadership.  The team’s win was also significant, however, because it demonstrated the resilience of the players and the team’s commitment to playing as a unit.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'random_id_a1',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"—The Sound of Silence—","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"—The Sound of Silence,","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The Sound of Silence","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The Sound of Silence—","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The convention being tested is the punctuation of a supplementary element within a sentence. The correct answer is C because the title of the album is an essential part of the sentence. In this case, the correct punctuation to separate the supplementary element from the rest of the sentence is a comma. This choice avoids a run-on sentence and is the only choice that uses punctuation correctly.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The musician’s new album, _The Sound of Silence_, is a collection of instrumental pieces that explore themes of solitude, reflection, and the beauty of nature. The album was recorded in a small studio in the countryside, and the musician’s goal was to create a sound that was both intimate and expansive. The album’s title reflects the musician’s desire to capture the quiet moments of contemplation that are often overlooked in the hustle and bustle of everyday life.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'f890dc20',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"waterways and the fact that,","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"waterways, and the fact that,","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"waterways, and, the fact that","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"waterways and the fact that","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is punctuation within two coordinated noun phrases. When, as in this case, a noun phrase ("the country’s millions of miles of waterways") is coordinated with another noun phrase ("the fact") followed by an integrated relative clause ("that the volume...time"), no punctuation is needed.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'Gathering accurate data on water flow in the United States is challenging because of the country’s millions of miles of ______ the volume and speed of water at any given location can vary drastically over time.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    '54c1b2dd',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'In the passage, the author suggests that the speaker’s use of the word "only" is a sign of a lack of sophistication. What does the author mean by this? ',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The speaker’s language is too formal and academic for the context.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The speaker is not being sincere in his or her feelings.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The speaker’s argument is not well-supported by evidence.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The speaker’s use of language is too simple and straightforward for the context.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'The author is suggesting that the speaker’s use of the word "only" is too simple and straightforward for the context, and that a more sophisticated speaker would use more nuanced language.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author describes the use of the word “only” as a sign of a “lack of sophistication.” What does the author mean by this statement?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Expression of Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Expression of Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Expression of Ideas']::text[],
    '5f75c25a',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Expression of Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice provides the most effective transition between the two sentences in the second underlined portion?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"during his last visit to Paris","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"while he was in Paris last","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"because he was returning to Paris for","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"on his most recent trip to Paris","is_correct":true,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'D',
    'Choice D is the most effective transition because it connects the two sentences smoothly and logically. The phrase ''on his most recent trip to Paris'' provides a clear connection between the artist''s previous visits to Paris and his decision to visit the Louvre.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'Although he had visited Paris many times, the artist had never been to the Louvre, *the most famous museum in the world*.  He had always been drawn to smaller, more intimate galleries, where the crowds were less overwhelming, and the artwork was more approachable.  The artist, however, decided to make a special trip to the Louvre *when his last visit to Paris*.  He was *excited to see* the Mona Lisa and to experience the museum’s vast collection of art.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'random_id_e1',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'What is the most likely reason why the discovery of the wave-particle duality of light did not provide a complete or final answer to the question of what light is?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The wave-particle duality of light only applies to certain types of light, not all types of light.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The discovery of the wave-particle duality of light was a significant step forward in understanding the nature of light, but there is still much that is not known about light.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The discovery of the wave-particle duality of light was made by a single scientist, and other scientists are still debating its validity.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The discovery of the wave-particle duality of light was based on flawed experimental evidence, so its validity is uncertain.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The passage states that the discovery of the wave-particle duality of light was a significant step forward in understanding the nature of light, but it also states that this discovery did not provide a complete or final answer to the question of what light is. This suggests that the discovery is a step on a path to a more complete understanding of light. Thus, the most likely reason why the passage states that the discovery of the wave-particle duality of light did not provide a complete or final answer is that there is still much that is not known about light.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The passage describes a time when scientists were struggling to understand the nature of light. Scientists knew that light traveled in a straight line, but they were unsure of what light actually was. Some scientists believed that light was a wave, while others believed that light was a particle.  The passage states that the discovery of the wave-particle duality of light provided a new understanding of the nature of light, but it also states that this discovery “did not provide a complete or final answer” to the question of what light is. 

What is the most likely reason why the passage states that the discovery of the wave-particle duality of light did not provide a complete or final answer to the question of what light is?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'random_id_c7',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author is primarily concerned with establishing that',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"the media plays a significant role in shaping public opinion.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"it is difficult to assess the precise impact of the media on public opinion.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"the media is a powerful tool for influencing public opinion.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"the media should be used responsibly to avoid distorting public opinion.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The main point of the passage is that the diverse nature of media sources makes it difficult to pinpoint precisely how it impacts public opinion. This is best expressed by choice B.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'It is difficult to assess the impact of the media on the public without considering the vast array of sources that contribute to the formation of public opinion. People are constantly bombarded with information from various sources, including social media, newspapers, television, and even word of mouth. Each of these sources has its own unique perspective and can influence the way people understand and interpret events. In addition, the media landscape is constantly evolving, with new platforms and technologies emerging all the time.  The dynamic nature of media, therefore, makes it difficult to pinpoint precisely how it impacts public opinion.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    '9d8c6671',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author mentions Carlyle’s work as a poet, translator, and social critic to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"contrast Carlyle’s historical writing with his work in other genres.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"emphasize the range and complexity of Carlyle’s talents.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"suggest that Carlyle’s historical work was influenced by his other writing.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"show how Carlyle’s work as a historian is informed by his social criticism.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The passage emphasizes the range and complexity of Carlyle''s talents, noting that he was a historian, poet, translator, and social critic. This demonstrates that Carlyle was a skilled and versatile writer.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    '“The history of the world is the history of the struggle between liberty and authority,” wrote the Scottish historian Thomas Carlyle in 1840. Although he is most famous for his work as a historian, Carlyle was also a poet, a translator, and a social critic.  In his writings, he often embraced a style that emphasized the power and beauty of language. What is the most likely reason the author mentions Carlyle’s work as a poet, translator, and social critic?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Craft and Structure']::text[],
    'random_id_b8',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Craft and Structure","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'What is the main idea of the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The author is suggesting that the research process for a complex topic can be both difficult and rewarding.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The author is describing how research is a constant process of discovery and analysis.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The author is outlining the importance of a clear research framework when working on a complex topic.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The author is highlighting the challenges of navigating conflicting perspectives in academic research.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The passage states that the author found the research process to be both "daunting" and "exciting" and ultimately "rewarding." This suggests that while the research process was challenging, it was ultimately worthwhile for the author.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author is writing a nonfiction book about a particularly complex and challenging topic. In the following passage, the author describes the research process for the book.  

"My initial research began with a deep dive into the existing body of academic work on this topic, and I found myself overwhelmed by the sheer number of studies, theories, and conflicting perspectives. The sheer volume of information was both daunting and exciting, for it revealed the breadth and complexity of the issue. I found myself needing to develop a framework to organize and understand the various approaches.  I tried to identify the key questions at the heart of the debate and to analyze how different scholars had addressed these questions. The process was a struggle at times, but it was ultimately rewarding."',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'a8d9d83d',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author includes the example of domain names to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"show the potential drawbacks of choosing a domain name that is too long or too complicated.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"explain how the domain name for a company should be chosen.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"demonstrate that choosing the right domain name is important.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"suggest that a company should choose a domain name that is relevant to its brand.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The example of domain names is given to illustrate the potential drawbacks of choosing a domain name that is too long or too complicated, as such a domain name would be difficult for customers to recall.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The first step in designing a website for a new company is choosing a domain name. Choosing the right domain name can be tricky;  it is important to make sure that the domain name is available, easy to remember, and relevant to the company’s brand.  A domain name that is too long or too complicated can be difficult for customers to recall. For example,  a company that sells home goods might choose a domain name such as “homegoods.com”  or “homegoodsshop.com” rather than a domain name such as “homegoodsemporiumandwarehouse.com.”  What is the most likely reason why the author includes the example of domain names in the text?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    '86a2d34c',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english"}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice provides the best punctuation for the underlined portion of the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"It is a powerful and moving story, about Angelou’s childhood and adolescence, and it explores themes of racism, sexism, and violence.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"It is a powerful and moving story; about Angelou’s childhood and adolescence, and it explores themes of racism, sexism, and violence.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"It is a powerful and moving story about Angelou’s childhood and adolescence, and it explores themes of racism, sexism, and violence.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"It is a powerful and moving story about Angelou’s childhood and adolescence, and it explores themes of racism, sexism, and violence;","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The underlined portion is a non-essential clause that adds information about the subject of the sentence. Non-essential clauses are set off with commas. The comma after “adolescence” separates the non-essential clause from the rest of the sentence, and the comma after “violence” separates the non-essential clause from the following independent clause.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The poet Maya Angelou’s autobiography, I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings, is an important work of literature. It is a powerful and moving story about Angelou’s childhood and adolescence, and it explores themes of racism, sexism, and violence.  The book is also significant because it was one of the first autobiographies by a Black woman that was widely read and praised.',
    'passage',

    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author emphasizes the significance of the word "real" by using several rhetorical devices.     'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'In 1994, the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) created the "Food Guide Pyramid" to help Americans understand how to make healthy food choices.     '[{"id":"A","text":"Many animals communicate with each other using a variety of sounds, scents, and visual signals; for example, birds may use songs.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"Many animals communicate with each other using a variety of sounds, scents, and visual signals, for example; birds may use songs.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"Many animals communicate with each other using a variety of sounds, scents, and visual signals; birds may use songs, insects may use pheromones, and chimpanzees may use facial expressions.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"Many animals communicate with each other using a variety of sounds; scents, and visual signals, for example, birds may use songs.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The semicolon is used correctly in choice A to separate two independent clauses. The other choices are incorrect because the semicolon is used incorrectly to separate two phrases (choice B), to separate more than two clauses (choice C), or to separate a phrase from a clause (choice D).',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
